generatedFor: future coding agents onboarding onto this repo — read this before exploring the codebase blind

project.name: Pokemon Trainer Companion (PTC)
project.tagline: Real-time AI-powered overlay for Pokemon GO
project.flagshipFeature: A native Android floating window that reads the screen live (MediaProjection + on-device OCR) and shows species/CP/IV/PvP tips over the game, with an optional Gemini-backed AI layer. Everything else (Pokedex, calculators, rankings) is the foundation this sits on.
project.status: v1.0 Beta, closed testing with friends
project.license: MIT (source code only — Pokemon IP not covered)

repoRoots.buildPath: C:\ptc (short path, required on Windows — MAX_PATH 260 chars breaks CMake/ninja otherwise)
repoRoots.githubSyncedClone: C:\Users\Leonardo Build\Documents\GitHub\pokemon-trainer-companion (kept in sync via commit+push from C:\ptc, then git pull here)

mobile.framework: React Native 0.86.0, bare workflow (not Expo — needed for custom native overlay module + ML Kit OCR)
mobile.state: Local only (useState/useMemo/useRef) — no Redux/Zustand, static data loaded from bundled JSON
mobile.i18n: Custom lightweight system in src/i18n/ — typed dictionary + React Context, EN/PT-BR/ES
mobile.networking: fetch() only, used exclusively by src/data/companion/companionApiClient.ts — the ONLY network calls in the whole mobile app; everything else is offline-first
mobile.ocr: @react-native-ml-kit/text-recognition (JS bridge) for gallery-picker flow; native ScreenCaptureService.kt calls the same ML Kit Android SDK directly in Kotlin for the live overlay loop

backend.orm: Prisma 7 (schema-first; migrations exist but NOT required — backend works fully without DATABASE_URL, PrismaService logs a warning and JSON-backed features still work)
backend.database: PostgreSQL 16 (optional — only needed for future Trainer/SavedTeam persistence, not yet wired to any endpoint)
backend.hosting: Render.com free tier — https://pokemon-trainer-companion.onrender.com/api (sleeps after 15 min idle, ~30s cold start)
backend.ai: Google Gemini via direct REST calls (no SDK dependency), default model gemini-flash-lite-latest (free tier, 1500 req/day, no credit card). Configured via GEMINI_API_KEY env var; missing key degrades /api/companion/* to a clean 503, never a crash.

native.language: Kotlin (the only non-TypeScript code in the repo)
native.purpose: Floating overlay window + live screen capture — the flagship feature's native half

architectureLayers.pattern: Clean Architecture on both mobile and backend

layers.name: domain
layers.description: Pure business logic, zero framework dependency (no RN, no NestJS decorators). Fully unit-testable in isolation.

layers.name: use-cases
layers.description: Orchestrates domain logic for a specific application flow.

layers.name: data
layers.description: Repository implementations, bundled JSON data, external API clients.

layers.name: presentation
layers.description: Mobile: screens/components/theme/navigation. Backend: controllers, DTOs, interceptors.

screens: PokemonDetailScreen (stats, PvP moveset, lore, evolution link)
TypeChartScreen (now wrapped in a type-color gradient via getTypeGradient)
OverlayDemoScreen (native overlay controls + gallery-screenshot analysis demo)
ToolsHubScreen, MoreScreen (dark 'PokeDex terminal' phosphor-green identity, see designSystem below)
keyComponents: CompanionWidget.tsx — floating draggable avatar, rule-based tips + Ask AI + Professor Mode entry point, rendered once at App root inside NavigationContainer
QuickActionsFab.tsx + BottomSheetMenu.tsx — Pokedex screen's floating action button + shortcut sheet
ErrorBoundary.tsx — app-wide crash fallback, wraps everything in App.tsx outside LanguageProvider
mobileModules.onlyNetworkCallSite: mobile/src/data/companion/companionApiClient.ts — every other feature is offline, reading bundled JSON in mobile/src/data/

designSystem.tokens: mobile/src/presentation/theme/colors.ts, typography.ts, spacing.ts, shadows.ts — hand-rolled design tokens, no styling library
designSystem.twoIdentities: Most screens use the light glass-card look (COLORS.surface/glassSurface/brandBlue etc). Utility-feeling screens (ToolsHubScreen, MoreScreen) were reworked into a deliberate second identity — a dark 'PokeDex terminal' look (inkBlack/inkPanel/phosphor/phosphorDim/phosphorGlow/inkText tokens, RETRO_FONT eyebrows, '> ' prompt accents on card titles) so those screens read as one distinctive brand moment instead of generic flat cards.
designSystem.typeColorReuse: getTypeGradient(typeColor) (colors.ts) mixes a Pokemon type's color toward white/black for a 2-stop gradient. Originally only used on PokemonDetailScreen; now also drives TypeChartScreen's full-screen background (keyed to the selected attacking type) and EvolutionChainScreen's per-card accent bar (keyed to each link's own type, since one chain can span multiple types).
designSystem.monoFont: typography.ts exports MONO_FONT (Platform.OS === 'ios' ? 'Courier' : 'monospace') — introduced to replace repeated inline Platform ternaries in ProfessorChatScreen's markdown code blocks.

routes: GET /system/version (data freshness / version metadata, see backend/src/data/dataVersion.ts)

dataSources.species: backend/src/data/species/ — static, PokeAPI + PoGo API sourced, 965 species + 166 regional/alternate forms (1131 total, mobile-side, via speciesForms.ts / forms-data.json merged into the base dataset) / Gen 1+2 range (backend companion grounding)
dataSources.raids: backend/src/data/raids/ — curated mock boss rotation (11 Gen 1 bosses, tiers 1/3/5), NOT live-synced
dataSources.knowledge: backend/src/data/knowledge/ — PokeAPI-sourced genus/habitat/flavor-text facts for all 251 Gen 1+2 species, grounds the Companion AI's prompts. Ingested via backend/scripts/fetchKnowledgeBase.mjs (one-off script, re-run to extend generations).

backendModules.prismaSchema: backend/prisma/schema.prisma — Trainer, SavedTeam, Species, Type models defined but NOT wired to any endpoint yet (cross-device sync is post-beta, blocked on Google Sign-In OAuth credentials)

files.OverlayModule.kt: React Native bridge module. Methods: hasOverlayPermission, requestOverlayPermission, showOverlay, hideOverlay, updateOverlayText, requestScreenCapturePermission, startLiveCapture, captureLiveFrame, stopLiveCapture. Emits events PTCOverlayTapped and PTCOverlayFrameText via a static ReactApplicationContext holder (reactApplicationContextHolder) so ScreenCaptureService can reach JS without an Activity reference.
files.ScreenCaptureService.kt: Foreground service (foregroundServiceType=mediaProjection). Opens the real MediaProjection session, VirtualDisplay + ImageReader for frame capture, and runs its OWN native polling loop (startPolling, ~4s interval) that grabs a frame, runs ML Kit OCR directly in Kotlin, and emits recognized text to JS. This loop is native specifically so it keeps running while the RN Activity is backgrounded — a JS-side setInterval doing the same thing was found to stall once the app lost foreground.

nativeOverlayModule.jsBridge: mobile/src/data/overlay/overlayBridge.ts — thin wrapper, every function is a safe no-op on iOS or if the native module isn't linked
flowSummary: 1. requestOverlayPermission() — user grants SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW via system settings screen
2. requestScreenCapturePermission() — Android's real MediaProjectionManager consent dialog
3. startLiveCapture() — starts ScreenCaptureService, opens MediaProjection session, begins native polling
4. JS listens via onOverlayFrameText(text) — analyzeOcrText(text) from mobile/src/use-cases/analyzeScreenshot.ts runs species lookup + IV/PvP/bulk analysis (same pipeline as gallery-picker flow)
5. updateOverlayText(body) pushes a formatted result (species/CP/IV/PvP league/bulk/tip) into the floating window

6. On first detection of a NEW species, an async fetchCompanionSuggestion() call upgrades the rule-based tip to a Gemini-generated one (only once per species, not every poll tick, to bound cost/latency)
7. Tapping the overlay brings PTC to foreground (packageManager launch intent) and emits PTCOverlayTapped, which OverlayDemoScreen listens for and navigates to ProfessorChat
knownGotchas: All WindowManager/view mutations MUST run via Handler(Looper.getMainLooper()) — native module methods execute on RN's own native-modules thread, not the true Android UI thread; the view is attached from whichever thread calls showOverlay(), and any later mutation from a different thread throws CalledFromWrongThreadException
MediaProjection.registerCallback(...) must be called before createVirtualDisplay() on newer Android versions or it throws IllegalStateException

ML Kit's text-recognition Android artifact is only an `implementation` dependency of @react-native-ml-kit/text-recognition's own build.gradle, so it is NOT compile-visible to the app module by default — app/build.gradle needs its own explicit `implementation("com.google.mlkit:text-recognition:16.0.1")` for ScreenCaptureService.kt to call the API directly

Testing 'share one app' MediaProjection consent from within the app that requested it is awkward — that shared app only produces frames while it's actually in the foreground, which conflicts with wanting to observe results from the requesting app's own UI. 'Share entire screen' avoids this for manual testing.

standingConstraintsForAgents: Keep the app lightweight — justify any new native dependency before adding one; prefer pure-JS libraries when they exist (e.g. react-native-markdown-display chosen specifically for having zero native code)
Prefer `adb install` over USB for physical-device testing rather than sharing APK files around

On 'usb conectado'/reconnect: check `adb devices` + Metro's http://localhost:8081/status BEFORE launching the app, not after hitting a red error screen — re-run `adb reverse tcp:8081 tcp:8081` and `tcp:3000 tcp:3000` every time
Always update README.md's progress table AND a relevant doc after implementing a feature — not just when reminded
Any change under mobile/android/ requires a full Gradle rebuild + reinstall; Metro's JS hot-reload does not pick up native changes

Never claim a UI fix works without live verification on-device/emulator — this codebase has a documented history of fixes that passed typecheck/tests but failed on a real device (see docs/debug-log.md)
Google Sign-In implementation is blocked pending the project owner creating OAuth client credentials in Google Cloud Console — do not attempt this on their behalf
GEMINI_API_KEY lives only in backend/.env (gitignored) — never commit it or expose it elsewhere
postBetaRoadmap: Feed live analysis results deeper inside the floating window itself (today: short species/CP/PvP/bulk/tip summary; full moveset/evolution detail still lives in-app)
Expand knowledge base past Gen 2 + richer community-sourced (Bulbapedia-style) facts
Cross-device sync + Google Sign-In auth (blocked on OAuth credentials)
Dark mode (COLORS is a static object imported by every screen's StyleSheet.create() — real theming needs a refactor to a reactive hook across every call site)
Migrate backend off Render free tier (Railway/Fly.io) to remove the 15-min sleep
Type effectiveness chart UI polish, Swagger docs exposure at /api/docs, test coverage across all 965 species
